title: Hoya ischnopus
intro: Hoya ischnopus is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as a type of waxy climber or wax plant. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hoya ischnopus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hoya ischnopus is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or syrup to treat various health conditions, and is also used as a topical application for skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Hoya ischnopus belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. The genus Hoya is a type of flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. It is often referred to as the "wax plant" or "porcelain flower." Hoya ischnopus is a species within this genus and is native to Southeast Asia.
